title: senna hookeriana
intro: Senna hookeriana, also known as the Hooker's senna, is a shrub-like perennial plant native to the Himalayan region. It belongs to the Fabaceae family and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its laxative effects due to its high content of anthraquinone glycosides. The plant has been used for centuries to treat various health issues, including constipation, digestive problems, and fever, thanks to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Senna hookeriana can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or capsule, and is often used in herbal medicine to promote digestive health and relieve symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome.
